hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Turn — 4. You checked. Your opponent bet — bigger than expected.

wrongReasons: 5 4 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with 5 4.
Q J makes a straight here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
8 6 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only high card wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 9 4

Your opponent called the flop with one pair, then picked up two pair on the turn. The sudden aggression on the turn — after passivity on the flop — is the tell: they improved and immediately started building the pot.
